title: Single and Double Shear Test
aim: To determine shear strength of rock under single and double shear conditions.
theory: Shear test determines resistance of rock against shear forces.
In single shear, failure occurs along one plane.
In double shear, failure occurs along two planes.
Shear stress is calculated as load divided by shear area.
Double shear provides higher strength due to two resisting planes.
The test helps evaluate cohesion and frictional resistance.
Used in design of rock structures and foundations.
It simulates conditions where rocks slide over each other.
procedure: Prepare the rock specimen with required dimensions.
Place the specimen in the shear box.
Align properly for single shear condition.
Apply load gradually until failure occurs.
Repeat the test for double shear setup.
Calculate shear stress for both cases.
Compare results of single and double shear.
calculation: For double shear, effective area is doubled
result: Shear strength in double shear is higher than single shear for the given rock.
